expected: Explains the skill, maturity levels, lifecycle stages, people/process alignment, and the assessment artifact workflow. Does not produce JSON.

input: /ai-dlc-maturity We are using AI in our SDLC. Are we doing it the right way?
expected: Creates a fillable Markdown assessment intake artifact in the response covering scope, lifecycle coverage, people/process, engineering controls, traceability, security, cost, evidence, and concerns. Does not assess yet unless enough context is provided.

input: /ai-dlc-maturity Please prepare a Markdown assessment artifact for our platform team to fill out.
expected: Creates or offers a reusable Markdown intake artifact. If file write access is available and the user clearly asks to prepare it as a file, writes it under outputs/ and explains how to continue after it is filled.

input: /ai-dlc-maturity Our team uses AI coding assistants for implementation and unit test drafting. Developers review PRs manually. CI runs tests and linting, but prompts and agent rules are personal, not versioned. We do not track token cost or trace shipped changes back to intent.
expected: Scores the team conservatively, identifies people/process gaps, traceability gaps, cost gaps, and recommends versioned rules, evidence capture, CI gates, and cost attribution. Includes inline JSON.

input: Continue the AI-DLC maturity assessment from this completed intake: [filled Markdown answers]
expected: Assesses the completed artifact, produces maturity card, lifecycle and dimension scores, people/process findings, top gaps, priority actions, evidence gaps, assumptions, and inline JSON.
